NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE ALONG A SPORADIC ZONE 1.5 MILES LONG. THE DAMAGE CONSISTED PRIMARILY OF SEVERAL UP-ROOTED OAK TREES AS WELL AS NUMEROUS BROKEN BRANCHES. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 55-65 MPH.

BASED ON NUMEROUS VIDEOS FROM TRAINED SPOTTERS AND THE PUBLIC, NWS CONFIRMS A WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED ON LAKE JESUP JUST EAST OF SR 417. THE WATERSPOUT MOVED EAST FOR 2.75 MILES AND CAME ONSHORE BRIEFLY AS AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60-65 MPH.

NWS MELBOURNE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-2 TORNADO MOVED THROUGH NORTH DELAND WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 105-115 MPH. THE EF-2 DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO E WASHINGTON AVENUE BETWEEN ALABAMA AVE AND AMELIA AVE, THOUGH EF-1 DAMAGE WAS WIDESPREAD FROM WOODWARD AVENUE EAST ACROSS A DENSELY WOODED SUBURBAN AREA TOWARDS KENDRA AVENUE.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE MELBOURNE DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMS THAT A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE SUNSET HILLS SUBDIVISION EAST OFORANGE CITY. SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE DOWNED ON BOTH SIDES OF COUNTY ROAD 4101, BETWEEN SUNNYSIDE AVE AND E GRAVES AVE. A MOBILE HOMEWAS DESTROYED AFTER ITS ROOF CAVED IN AT THE INTERSECTION OF SUNNYSIVE AVE AND COUNTY ROAD 4101.
